perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS, the parties to this Agreement (each a “ ​ Party ​ ” and collectively, the “ ​ Parties ​ ”) have entered into discussions regarding a possible transaction or series of transactions between them relating to ___________________ (the “ ​ Transaction ​ ”), and in connection with such discussions, each Party has furnished or may furnish to the other Party and/or its Affiliates (as hereinafter defined) and their respective Representatives (as hereinafter defined) information regarding their products, services, technology, business, customers, clients, working capital, affairs, assets, condition (financial or otherwise) and/or prospects which is either non-public, confidential or proprietary in nature;
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS, the parties to this Agreement (each a “ ​ Party ​ ” and collectively, the “ ​ Individuals ​ ”) have entered into discussions regarding a possible transaction or series of transactions between them relating to ___________________ (the “ ​ Transaction ​ ”), and in connection with such discussions, each Party has furnished or may furnish to the other Party and/or its Affiliates (as hereinafter defined) and their respective Representatives (as hereinafter defined) information regarding their products, services, technology, business, customers, clients, working capital, affairs, assets, condition (financial or otherwise) and/or prospects which is either non-public, confidential or proprietary in nature;
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Parties' is replaced with 'Individuals'. This creates contradiction as the agreement defines 'Parties' as legal entities. Changing this introduces ambiguity and a clear contradiction. It's contradicting specifically on the definition of Parties vs the actual use of Individuals.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS, the parties to this Agreement (each a “ ​ Party ​ ” and collectively, the “ ​ Individuals ​ ”) have entered into discussions regarding a possible transaction or series of transactions between them relating to ___________________ (the “ ​ Transaction ​ ”), and in connection with such discussions, each Party has furnished or may furnish to the other Party and/or its Affiliates (as hereinafter defined) and their respective Representatives (as hereinafter defined) information regarding their products, services, technology, business, customers, clients, working capital, affairs, assets, condition (financial or otherwise) and/or prospects which is either non-public, confidential or proprietary in nature;
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Parties' with 'Individuals' while the contradicted text refers to 'Parties', creating a direct contradiction. This is because the agreement defines 'Parties' as distinct from 'Individuals'. This misalignment of terminology, as explained, introduces ambiguity and uncertainty, making it unclear whether the agreement applies to legal entities or natural persons. This is a strong in-text contradiction as per the Misaligned Terminology definition provided.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential ​ Information ​ ” means all information, whether visual, written, electronic or oral, (including any information furnished prior to the execution of this Agreement) related to the products, services, technology, business, customers, working capital, affairs, assets, condition (financial or otherwise) or prospects of a Disclosing Party which is furnished by such Disclosing Party, or otherwise disclosed by such Disclosing Party, to a Receiving Party in connection with the consideration and evaluation of the Transaction (including, without limitation, samples, compounds and ingredients thereof, prototypes, formulae, technical know how, technical data, financial information, payment terms, performance data, product specifications and customer and/or supplier information) and all reports, analyses, compilations, data, studies and other materials (in whatever form maintained, whether documentary, computer storage or otherwise)
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential ​ Information ​ ” means only written information furnished after the execution of this Agreement directly related to specific financial details of the transaction. This includes only the final financial reports and excludes all preliminary discussions, analyses, and supporting documentation.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Confidential Information' is restricted. Initially, it included a wide range of information; now it's limited to final financial reports after the agreement, excluding earlier discussions and support. This contradicts the intent that all information shared regarding the transaction should be confidential. Contradicts section 2 (Use of Confidential Information) where it says each receiving party shall keep all confidential information it receives or obtains from a disclosing party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Receiving Party shall keep all Confidential Information it receives or obtains from a Disclosing Party confidential and shall not, without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party, disclose such Confidential Information, in whole or in part, and shall not use such Confidential Information, directly or indirectly, for any purpose other than in connection with evaluating the Transaction. The exposure of the Receiving Party to the Confidential Information or its disclosure shall not be construed in any way to grant to the Receiving Party any right or license with respect to the Information other than the right to use such Confidential Information strictly in accordance with this Agreement. Moreover, each Receiving Party shall have the right Confidentiality Non-Disclosure Agreement 9 Page 2 of 6 Rev. 08-Oct-18 to disclose that it is evaluating the Transaction and transmit Confidential Information to its Representatives only if and to the extent that such Representatives need to know the Confidential Information for the purpose of evaluating the Transaction and are informed by such Receiving Party of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and agree (in writing) to be bound by the terms of this Agreement. Each Party shall be responsible for any actions by its Affiliates and/or Representatives which are not in accordance with the agreements herein regarding the conduct of its Affiliates and/or Representatives. Visitors shall not take photographs or otherwise record the activities at the other party’s facilities.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly restricts the definition of 'Confidential Information' to only written financial reports after the agreement, while the original definition encompassed a broad range of information. The contradicted text states that each receiving party shall keep *all* confidential information it receives confidential. Since the changed text limits what constitutes 'Confidential Information', it directly contradicts the contradicted text's requirement to keep a broader scope of information confidential, because information that was initially confidential is no longer considered such. Therefore, a contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Each Receiving Party shall keep all Confidential Information it receives or obtains from a Disclosing Party confidential and shall not, without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party, disclose such Confidential Information, in whole or in part, and shall not use such Confidential Information, directly or indirectly, for any purpose other than in connection with evaluating the Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Receiving Party shall endeavor to keep Confidential Information it receives or obtains from a Disclosing Party confidential and shall not, whenever possible, disclose such Confidential Information, in whole or in part, and shall only use such Confidential Information to the extent necessary for evaluating the Transaction.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall keep' to 'shall endeavor to keep' weakens the obligation. 'Shall not' becomes 'shall not whenever possible', further weakening the restriction on disclosure. 'Any purpose other than' is altered to 'only to the extent necessary'. This creates loopholes because the original terms establish firm obligations, whereas the altered terms permit subjective interpretation. This undermines the core premise of the NDA. It is contradicting with the Equitable Relief. The original text says each party acknowledges that the other Party would be irreparably injured by a breach of this agreement, if the definition is changed in the section 2(a) then that is creating a contradiction in section 4
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party acknowledges that the other Party would be irreparably injured by a breach of this Agreement by a Receiving Party or its Affiliates or Representatives and that each Party shall be entitled to equitable relief, including injunctive relief and specific performance, in the event of any breach of the provisions of this Agreement by any of the other Parties. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement, but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the obligation of the Receiving Party to protect Confidential Information by changing 'shall keep' to 'shall endeavor to keep' and 'shall not' to 'shall not whenever possible'. This introduces subjective interpretation and creates loopholes, undermining the firm obligations established in the original text. Because the original text was an agreement about the need for Equitable Relief, and now the original intent is contradicted, the new changes do in fact change the meaning of the original contract. Therefore, the original meaning of keeping all confidential information has been contradicted and it is in YES.

perturbation.original_text: Promptly following the request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall, as directed by the Disclosing Party, (i) destroy (and confirm such destruction in writing to the Disclosing Party) or (ii) return all Information it receives or obtains from a Disclosing Party and all copies thereof, as well as all other materials containing Confidential Information in the Receiving Party’s possession (including, without limitation, notes), without retaining any copies or extracts thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the termination of discussions, the Receiving Party may, at its discretion, destroy or return some of the Information it receives or obtains from a Disclosing Party and some copies thereof. The Receiving Party may keep notes, analysis and other materials that have been made by the Receiving Party for internal purposes.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'Promptly following the request' to 'Upon the termination of discussions' changes the timing. 'Shall' is replaced with 'may', making destruction/return optional. The receiving party can now keep notes, analysis, and other materials for internal purposes, this is in direct contradiction with the original 'without retaining any copies'. It is directly contradicting with section 5, because in the original text, no license, intellectual property right or other ownership or use right is conveyed solely by this Agreement, but with the retention of the copy, there could be a use right
perturbation.contradicted_text: No license, intellectual property right or other ownership or use right is conveyed solely by this Agreement, except the limited right to use Confidential Information described in Sections 1 and 2 of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a strong contradiction. The original text mandates the destruction or return of all confidential information and prohibits retaining any copies. The changed text allows the Receiving Party to optionally destroy or return *some* information and retain notes/analysis for internal purposes. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which states that no license or use right is conveyed except the limited right to use confidential information described in Sections 1 and 2. By retaining copies and potentially using them internally, the Receiving Party effectively gains a use right not explicitly granted in the original agreement, thus creating a strong, in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Company agrees that, for the term of this Agreement and for two (2) years following its termination, Company will not directly or indirectly, solicit or assist others in soliciting, or attempt to solicit or assist others in attempting to solicit, (i) any existing customers, clients, suppliers, business partners, and/or vendors of Chief Enterprises with or about whom the Company obtained Confidential Information, or (ii) any prospective customers, clients, suppliers, business partners, and/or vendors of Chief Enterprises with or about whom the Company obtained Confidential Information and with whom Chief Enterprises took significant steps to do business with during the term of this Agreement, for the purpose or effect of inducing such existing or prospective customers, clients, suppliers, business partners, and/or vendors to cease doing business or reduce their business with Chief Enterprises or to purchase, lease, or utilize products or services that are competitive with, similar to, or that may be used as substitutes for any products or services offered by Chief Enterprises.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company agrees that, for the term of this Agreement and for one (1) year following its termination, Company will not directly solicit any existing customers of Chief Enterprises with or about whom the Company obtained Confidential Information. This restriction does not apply to prospective customers or any customers who independently seek out the Company's services.
perturbation.explanation: The scope of non-solicitation is reduced, from 2 years to 1 year. The restrictions now only apply to direct solicitation of *existing* customers and exclude 'clients, suppliers, business partners, and/or vendors' and are excluding prospective customers, which opens up potential loopholes where solicitation through indirect channels is permissible. it is contradicting section 4, where it says that each party would be irreparably injured by a breach of this Agreement
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party acknowledges that the other Party would be irreparably injured by a breach of this Agreement by a Receiving Party or its Affiliates or Representatives and that each Party shall be entitled to equitable relief, including injunctive relief and specific performance, in the event of any breach of the provisions of this Agreement by any of the other Parties. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement, but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly reduces the scope and duration of the non-solicitation agreement, limiting it to one year and only direct solicitation of existing customers. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that *any* breach of the agreement would cause irreparable harm. The change makes it less clear if a breach has occurred and when, leading to uncertainty regarding enforcement and whether 'irreparable injury' would occur in scenarios now permitted by the changed text.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be in effect for a period of two (2) years from the date hereof, provided that (i) either Party may terminate this Agreement by giving 60 days’ written notice in accordance with the notice provisions hereof and (ii) with respect to Confidential Information disclosed hereunder, the confidentiality obligations set forth in Paragraph 2(a) shall remain in full force and effect for so long as such Confidential Information remains confidential and non-public.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be in effect indefinitely from the date hereof, unless terminated by either party with 30 days written notice. Confidentiality obligations shall cease upon termination regardless of whether information remains confidential.
perturbation.explanation: The agreement's term changes from two years to indefinite, contradicting the original timeframe. Moreover, the obligation for confidentiality ceases upon termination regardless of the public nature of the information, and this creates an in-text contradiction, because the original agreements specifies that confidentiality stays in effect so long as it is Confidential Information remains confidential and non-public.. This can contradict Equitable relief, because it can affect the other party if confidentiality ends abruptly, which it wouldn't have under normal conditions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party acknowledges that the other Party would be irreparably injured by a breach of this Agreement by a Receiving Party or its Affiliates or Representatives and that each Party shall be entitled to equitable relief, including injunctive relief and specific performance, in the event of any breach of the provisions of this Agreement by any of the other Parties. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement, but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the agreement's term from two years to indefinite and removes the provision that confidentiality obligations remain in effect as long as the information remains confidential and non-public. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that parties are entitled to equitable relief for breaches of the agreement. Because confidentiality may now end abruptly regardless of the public nature of the information, it undermines the basis for equitable relief in the event of a confidentiality breach, creating a strong contradiction and potential unenforceability.
